reasoning: The resolution is based on the final 40dB barometer of 2025 for evaluating Vox's vote intention among 18-24 year olds. Polled figures recently were 29.9% (Dec '24), 20.7% (Jan '25), 27.4% (Feb '25), 24.9% (Jun '25), and 27.9% (Jul '25). The mean is ~26.1%. Because Vox reached 27.9% in July, and the overarching political trend favors right-wing momentum amid PSOE scandals, underlying support might be around 26-27%. With a standard error of ~3.5%, a true mean of ~26.5% translates to roughly a 45-50% chance of exceeding the 27.0% threshold purely via sampling variance, though the slight upward momentum pushes my final estimate slightly higher. The community is slightly higher (60%), but the historical occurrence reflects that missing the threshold (as in Jan and Jun) is just as normal.
